## Modified PVR
```{r}
###############################################################################
##  Sample Size Category vs Precision Gain — Boxplot only
##  Layout: 2 cols (Continuous, Binary) x 3 rows (All, Top-3, Baseline+)
##  Legends: bottom, one per column (continuous / binary)
###############################################################################

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(patchwork)
library(ggpattern)
library(cowplot)

## ---------- colour palettes ----------
method_fill_colors = c(
  "ANCOVA"     = "#F8766D",
  "ANHECOVA"   = "#B79F00",
  "G-Logistic" = "#00BA38",
  "DML"        = "#9FE8ED",
  "TMLE"       = "#B8D4FF",
  "IPW"        = "#C77CFF"
)

method_border_colors = c(
  "ANCOVA"     = "grey25",
  "ANHECOVA"   = "grey25",
  "G-Logistic" = "grey25",
  "DML"        = "grey25",
  "TMLE"       = "grey25",
  "IPW"        = "grey25"
)

method_linewidths = c(
  "ANCOVA"     = 0.35,
  "ANHECOVA"   = 0.35,
  "G-Logistic" = 0.35,
  "DML"        = 0.35,
  "TMLE"       = 0.35,
  "IPW"        = 0.35
)

method_patterns = c(
  "ANCOVA"     = "none",
  "ANHECOVA"   = "none",
  "G-Logistic" = "none",
  "DML"        = "none",
  "TMLE"       = "none",
  "IPW"        = "none"
)

method_pattern_angles = c(
  "ANCOVA"     = 0,
  "ANHECOVA"   = 0,
  "G-Logistic" = 0,
  "DML"        = 0,
  "TMLE"       = 0,
  "IPW"        = 0
)

## renamed x-axis labels
size_labels = c(
  "0-100"   = "Small\n(<100)",
  "100-500" = "Medium\n(100-500)",
  "500+"    = "Large\n(>500)"
)

###############################################################################
##  Categorize sample size
###############################################################################
categorize_sample_size = function(data) {
  data %>%
    mutate(size_cat = case_when(
      Sample_Size <= 100                     ~ "0-100",
      Sample_Size > 100 & Sample_Size <= 500 ~ "100-500",
      Sample_Size > 500                      ~ "500+"
    )) %>%
    mutate(size_cat = factor(size_cat, levels = c("0-100", "100-500", "500+")))
}

df_long_cat_all     = categorize_sample_size(df_long_size_cleaned_all)
df_long_cat_primary = categorize_sample_size(df_long_size_cleaned_primary)

###############################################################################
##  Helper: extract bottom legend grob for a given method set
###############################################################################
make_legend_grob = function(method_levels) {
  df_dummy = data.frame(
    x      = seq_along(method_levels),
    y      = rep(0, length(method_levels)),
    method = factor(method_levels, levels = method_levels)
  )

  p_leg = ggplot(df_dummy, aes(x = x, y = y,
                                fill          = method,
                                colour        = method,
                                pattern       = method,
                                pattern_angle = method,
                                linewidth     = method)) +
    ggpattern::geom_boxplot_pattern(
      pattern_density = 0.08,
      pattern_spacing = 0.04,
      pattern_size    = 0.4,
      pattern_colour  = "grey30",
      pattern_fill    = NA
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(values          = method_fill_colors[method_levels]) +
    scale_colour_manual(values        = method_border_colors[method_levels]) +
    scale_linewidth_manual(values     = method_linewidths[method_levels]) +
    scale_pattern_manual(values       = method_patterns[method_levels]) +
    scale_pattern_angle_manual(values = method_pattern_angles[method_levels]) +
    guides(
      colour        = "none",
      linewidth     = "none",
      pattern_angle = "none",
      pattern       = "none",
      fill          = guide_legend(
        title = "Method",
        nrow  = 1,
        override.aes = list(
          colour          = method_border_colors[method_levels],
          linewidth       = method_linewidths[method_levels],
          pattern         = method_patterns[method_levels],
          pattern_angle   = method_pattern_angles[method_levels],
          pattern_density = 0.08,
          pattern_spacing = 0.04,
          pattern_size    = 0.4,
          pattern_colour  = "grey30"
        ))
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.text     = element_text(size = 16),
      legend.title    = element_text(size = 18),
      legend.key.size = unit(0.9, "cm")
    )

  cowplot::get_legend(p_leg)
}

###############################################################################
##  BOXPLOT — one panel (legend always suppressed; extracted separately)
###############################################################################
make_boxplot_cat = function(data, outcome_type) {

  if (outcome_type == "binary") {
    method_levels = c("ANCOVA", "ANHECOVA", "G-Logistic", "DML", "TMLE", "IPW")
  } else {
    method_levels = c("ANCOVA", "ANHECOVA", "DML", "TMLE", "IPW")
    data      = data %>% filter(method != "G-Logistic")
  }

  # Unified box size so continuous and binary boxes appear the same width
  box_width = 0.63
  dodge_w   = 0.70

  data = data %>%
    filter(!is.na(size_cat), !is.na(prop_var_reduction)) %>%
    mutate(method   = factor(method, levels = method_levels),
           size_cat = factor(size_cat, levels = c("0-100", "100-500", "500+")))

  ggplot(data, aes(x = size_cat, y = prop_var_reduction,
                   fill          = method,
                   colour        = method,
                   pattern       = method,
                   pattern_angle = method,
                   linewidth     = method)) +
    ggpattern::geom_boxplot_pattern(
      position        = position_dodge(width = dodge_w),
      width           = box_width,
      outlier.shape   = NA,
      pattern_density = 0.08,
      pattern_spacing = 0.04,
      pattern_size    = 0.4,
      pattern_colour  = "grey30",
      pattern_fill    = NA
    ) +
    stat_summary(
      aes(group = method),
      fun         = median,
      geom        = "crossbar",
      width       = box_width,
      fatten      = 1,
      colour      = "black",
      linewidth   = 0.5,
      position    = position_dodge(width = dodge_w),
      show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40",
               linewidth = 0.7) +
    scale_fill_manual(values          = method_fill_colors[method_levels]) +
    scale_colour_manual(values        = method_border_colors[method_levels]) +
    scale_linewidth_manual(values     = method_linewidths[method_levels]) +
    scale_pattern_manual(values       = method_patterns[method_levels]) +
    scale_pattern_angle_manual(values = method_pattern_angles[method_levels]) +
    scale_x_discrete(labels = size_labels) +
    labs(x = "Sample Size", y = "Precision Gain") +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-1, 1)) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 20) +
    theme(
      legend.position    = "none",
      panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
      panel.background   = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
      plot.background    = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
      axis.text          = element_text(size = 18),
      axis.title         = element_text(size = 20),
      axis.text.x        = element_text(size = 15)
    )
}

###############################################################################
##  Assemble 2-col x 3-row grid with bottom legends per column
###############################################################################
assemble_grid_2x3 = function(data_source) {

  adj_configs = list(
    list(adj = "All",       label = "All"),
    list(adj = "Top-3",     label = "Top-3"),
    list(adj = "Baseline+", label = "Baseline+")
  )

  cont_panels = list()
  bin_panels  = list()

  method_levels_cont = c("ANCOVA", "ANHECOVA", "DML", "TMLE", "IPW")
  method_levels_bin  = c("ANCOVA", "ANHECOVA", "G-Logistic", "DML", "TMLE", "IPW")

  for (i in 1:3) {
    cfg = adj_configs[[i]]

    df_cont = data_source %>% filter(outcome_group == "continuous", adjustment_type == cfg$adj)
    df_bin  = data_source %>% filter(outcome_group == "binary",     adjustment_type == cfg$adj)

    p_cont = make_boxplot_cat(df_cont, "continuous")
    p_bin  = make_boxplot_cat(df_bin,  "binary")

    # column titles on top row only
    if (i == 1) {
      p_cont = p_cont + ggtitle("Continuous") +
        theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 19, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5))
      p_bin  = p_bin  + ggtitle("Binary") +
        theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 19, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5))
    }

    cont_panels[[i]] = p_cont
    bin_panels[[i]]  = p_bin
  }

  # Row labels
  row_labels = lapply(c("All", "Top-3", "Baseline+"), function(lbl) {
    wrap_elements(
      grid::textGrob(lbl, rot = 90,
                     gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 20, fontface = "bold"))
    )
  })

  # Legend grobs (one per column)
  leg_cont = make_legend_grob(method_levels_cont)
  leg_bin  = make_legend_grob(method_levels_bin)

  # Main panel grid
  main_grid = wrap_plots(
    row_labels[[1]], cont_panels[[1]], bin_panels[[1]],
    row_labels[[2]], cont_panels[[2]], bin_panels[[2]],
    row_labels[[3]], cont_panels[[3]], bin_panels[[3]],
    ncol   = 3,
    widths = c(0.05, 1, 1)
  )

  # Legend row: spacer | cont legend | bin legend
  leg_row = wrap_plots(
    plot_spacer(),
    wrap_elements(full = leg_cont),
    wrap_elements(full = leg_bin),
    ncol   = 3,
    widths = c(0.05, 1, 1)
  )

  # Stack
  main_grid / leg_row + plot_layout(heights = c(1, 0.08))
}

###############################################################################
##  Generate figures
###############################################################################
final_boxplot_all     = assemble_grid_2x3(df_long_cat_all)
final_boxplot_primary = assemble_grid_2x3(df_long_cat_primary)

###############################################################################
##  Save
###############################################################################
output_dir = "plots/samplesize_boxplot"
dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "samplesize_BOXPLOT_6panel_all.png"),
       final_boxplot_all, width = 22, height = 18, dpi = 600, bg = "white")
ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "samplesize_BOXPLOT_6panel_all.pdf"),
       final_boxplot_all, width = 22, height = 18, bg = "white")

ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "samplesize_BOXPLOT_6panel_primary.png"),
       final_boxplot_primary, width = 22, height = 18, dpi = 600, bg = "white")
ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "samplesize_BOXPLOT_6panel_primary.pdf"),
       final_boxplot_primary, width = 22, height = 18, bg = "white")

cat("Saved boxplot figures to:", output_dir, "\n")
```
## Modified Shift
```{r}
###############################################################################
##  ESTIMATE SHIFT — 2-Panel Figure (no method highlighting)
##  Panel 1: Continuous | Panel 2: Binary
##  X-axis: All, Top-3, Baseline+
##  Within each group: side-by-side boxplots per method
###############################################################################

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(patchwork)
library(cowplot)

## ---------- colour palette (uniform styling, no highlighting) ----------

method_colors = c(
  "ANCOVA"     = "#F8766D",
  "ANHECOVA"   = "#B79F00",
  "G-Logistic" = "#00BA38",
  "DML"        = "#00BFC4",
  "TMLE"       = "#619CFF",
  "IPW"        = "#C77CFF"
)

###############################################################################
##  Manual outlier threshold & selective removal
###############################################################################
diff_manual_threshold = 8

apply_selective_diff_outlier_removal = function(data) {
  data %>%
    mutate(diff = ifelse(
      method == "ANHECOVA" & adjustment_type == "All" & outcome_group == "continuous" & abs(diff) > diff_manual_threshold,
      NA_real_,
      diff
    ))
}

df_long_diff_selective_all     = apply_selective_diff_outlier_removal(df_long_diff_full_all)
df_long_diff_selective_primary = apply_selective_diff_outlier_removal(df_long_diff_full_primary)

###############################################################################
##  Helper: extract bottom legend grob for a given method set
###############################################################################
make_legend_grob = function(method_levels) {
  df_dummy = data.frame(
    x      = seq_along(method_levels),
    y      = rep(0, length(method_levels)),
    method = factor(method_levels, levels = method_levels)
  )

  p_leg = ggplot(df_dummy, aes(x = x, y = y, fill = method)) +
    geom_boxplot() +
    scale_fill_manual(values = method_colors[method_levels]) +
    guides(fill = guide_legend(title = "Method", nrow = 1)) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.text     = element_text(size = 16),
      legend.title    = element_text(size = 18),
      legend.key.size = unit(0.9, "cm")
    )

  cowplot::get_legend(p_leg)
}

###############################################################################
##  Plot function — one panel (continuous or binary), legend always off
###############################################################################
make_shift_panel = function(data, outcome_type) {

  if (outcome_type == "binary") {
    method_levels = c("ANCOVA", "ANHECOVA", "G-Logistic", "DML", "TMLE", "IPW")
    box_width = 0.63
    dodge_w   = 0.70
  } else {
    method_levels = c("ANCOVA", "ANHECOVA", "DML", "TMLE", "IPW")
    data      = data %>% filter(method != "G-Logistic")
    box_width = 0.63
    dodge_w   = 0.70
  }

  data = data %>%
    filter(!is.na(diff)) %>%
    mutate(
      method          = factor(method, levels = method_levels),
      adjustment_type = factor(adjustment_type, levels = c("All", "Top-3", "Baseline+"))
    )

  ggplot(data, aes(x = adjustment_type, y = diff, fill = method)) +
    geom_boxplot(
      colour        = "grey25",
      linewidth     = 0.4,
      position      = position_dodge(width = dodge_w),
      width         = box_width,
      outlier.shape = NA
    ) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40",
               linewidth = 0.7) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = method_colors[method_levels]) +
    labs(x = NULL, y = "Estimate Shift") +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-3, 3)) +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = c(-3, -1.5, 0, 1.5, 3)) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 20) +
    theme(
      legend.position    = "none",
      panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
      panel.background   = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
      plot.background    = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
      axis.text          = element_text(size = 18),
      axis.title         = element_text(size = 20),
      axis.text.x        = element_text(size = 17)
    )
}

###############################################################################
##  Assemble 2-panel figure with bottom legends per column
###############################################################################
assemble_shift = function(df_cont, df_bin) {

  method_levels_cont = c("ANCOVA", "ANHECOVA", "DML", "TMLE", "IPW")
  method_levels_bin  = c("ANCOVA", "ANHECOVA", "G-Logistic", "DML", "TMLE", "IPW")

  p_cont = make_shift_panel(df_cont, "continuous") +
    ggtitle("Continuous") +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 19, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5))

  p_bin = make_shift_panel(df_bin, "binary") +
    ggtitle("Binary") +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 19, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5))

  leg_cont = make_legend_grob(method_levels_cont)
  leg_bin  = make_legend_grob(method_levels_bin)

  main_row = p_cont + p_bin + plot_layout(widths = c(1, 1))

  leg_row = wrap_plots(
    wrap_elements(full = leg_cont),
    wrap_elements(full = leg_bin),
    ncol   = 2,
    widths = c(1, 1)
  )

  main_row / leg_row + plot_layout(heights = c(1, 0.08))
}

###############################################################################
##  Generate 2-panel figures
###############################################################################

## --- All outcomes ---
df_cont_all = df_long_diff_selective_all %>% filter(outcome_group == "continuous")
df_bin_all  = df_long_diff_selective_all %>% filter(outcome_group == "binary")
final_shift_all = assemble_shift(df_cont_all, df_bin_all)

## --- Primary outcomes ---
df_cont_primary = df_long_diff_selective_primary %>% filter(outcome_group == "continuous")
df_bin_primary  = df_long_diff_selective_primary %>% filter(outcome_group == "binary")
final_shift_primary = assemble_shift(df_cont_primary, df_bin_primary)

###############################################################################
##  Save
###############################################################################
output_dir = "plots/estimate_shift"
dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "estimate_shift_2panel_all.png"),
       final_shift_all, width = 20, height = 10, dpi = 600, bg = "white")
ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "estimate_shift_2panel_all.pdf"),
       final_shift_all, width = 20, height = 10, bg = "white")

ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "estimate_shift_2panel_primary.png"),
       final_shift_primary, width = 20, height = 10, dpi = 600, bg = "white")
ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "estimate_shift_2panel_primary.pdf"),
       final_shift_primary, width = 20, height = 10, bg = "white")

cat("Saved estimate shift figures to:", output_dir, "\n")
```


# Propensity Modeling
```{r}
# Manual outlier threshold for precision gain (same as main analysis)
pg_manual_threshold = 5

prepare_tmle_long_data = function(df_old, df_new, outcome_type, vs_method = "All") {
  
  # Filter by outcome type
  if (outcome_type == "continuous") {
    df_old_filtered = df_old %>%
      filter(`Outcome Type` %in% c("continuous", "continuous proportion"))
    df_new_filtered = df_new %>%
      filter(`Outcome Type` %in% c("continuous", "continuous proportion"))
  } else if (outcome_type == "binary") {
    df_old_filtered = df_old %>%
      filter(`Outcome Type` %in% c("binary", "composite binary"))
    df_new_filtered = df_new %>%
      filter(`Outcome Type` %in% c("binary", "composite binary"))
  }
  
  # Select the correct precision gain column based on VS method
  pg_col = switch(vs_method,
    "All"       = "How much precision gain can TMLE provide?",
    "Top3"      = "How much precision gain can TMLE_VS1 provide?",
    "Baseline+" = "How much precision gain can TMLE_VS2 provide?"
  )
  
  # Create dataframe for TMLE without PS
  tmle_without_ps = data.frame(
    method = "TMLE without PS",
    pg     = df_old_filtered[[pg_col]],
    pvr    = 1 - df_old_filtered[[pg_col]],
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  # Create dataframe for TMLE with PS
  tmle_with_ps = data.frame(
    method = "TMLE with PS",
    pg     = df_new_filtered[[pg_col]],
    pvr    = 1 - df_new_filtered[[pg_col]],
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  result = bind_rows(tmle_without_ps, tmle_with_ps)
  return(result)
}

###############################################################################
##  Color palette for TMLE comparison ----------------------------------------
###############################################################################
tmle_colors = c(
  "TMLE without PS" = "#619CFF",
  "TMLE with PS"    = "#F8766D"
)

###############################################################################
##  VS method display labels -------------------------------------------------
###############################################################################
vs_labels = c(
  "All"       = "All Covariates",
  "Top3"      = "Top-3 Correlation",
  "Baseline+" = "Baseline+"
)

###############################################################################
##  Plot function for TMLE comparison ----------------------------------------
###############################################################################
make_tmle_plot = function(data, outcome_type, vs_method) {
  
  data = data %>% filter(!is.na(pvr))
  
  data = data %>%
    mutate(method = factor(method, levels = c("TMLE without PS", "TMLE with PS")))
  
  method_counts = data %>%
    group_by(method) %>%
    summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop")
  
  x_labels_df = data.frame(
    method = c("TMLE without PS", "TMLE with PS"),
    x_pos  = 1:2
  )
  
  x_labels_counts = x_labels_df %>%
    left_join(method_counts, by = "method") %>%
    mutate(display_name = case_when(
      method == "TMLE without PS" ~ "TMLE\nwithout PS",
      method == "TMLE with PS"    ~ "TMLE\nwith PS"
    ))
  
  # Subtitle showing VS method
  plot_subtitle = paste0("Variable Selection: ", vs_labels[vs_method])
  
  p = ggplot(data, aes(method, pvr, fill = method)) +
    geom_boxplot(colour = "grey25", alpha = .50, width = 0.5, outlier.shape = NA) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = tmle_colors) +
    labs(x = NULL, y = "Proportional Variance Reduction",
         subtitle = plot_subtitle) +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-1, 1), clip = "off") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 18) +
    theme(
      axis.text.x      = element_blank(),
      axis.ticks.x     = element_blank(),
      axis.text.y      = element_text(size = 16),
      axis.title.y     = element_text(size = 18),
      plot.subtitle    = element_text(size = 14, colour = "grey30", hjust = 0.5),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
      plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
      legend.position  = "none",
      plot.margin      = margin(t = 5, r = 10, b = 60, l = 10)
    ) +
    geom_text(data = x_labels_counts,
              aes(x = x_pos, y = -1.15, label = display_name),
              inherit.aes = FALSE, size = 5.5, lineheight = 0.85, hjust = 0.5) +
    geom_text(data = x_labels_counts,
              aes(x = x_pos, y = -1.35, label = paste0("(", n, ")")),
              inherit.aes = FALSE, size = 4.5, hjust = 0.5)
  
  return(p)
}

###############################################################################
##  Publication quality settings ---------------------------------------------
###############################################################################
pub_width  = 8
pub_height = 6.5
pub_dpi    = 600

###############################################################################
##  Create output directory --------------------------------------------------
###############################################################################
output_dir = "cleaned_data/plot/exam plot"
dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

###############################################################################
##  Loop over all VS methods -------------------------------------------------
###############################################################################
vs_methods = c("All", "Top3", "Baseline+")

# Storage for summary stats
all_stats = list()

for (vs in vs_methods) {
  
  vs_tag = gsub("\\+", "plus", vs)   # safe for filenames: "Baseline+" -> "Baselineplus"
  vs_tag = tolower(vs_tag)
  
  cat("\n========================================\n")
  cat("VS Method:", vs_labels[vs], "\n")
  cat("========================================\n\n")
  
  # Prepare data
  df_binary     = prepare_tmle_long_data(df_comparison, df_newcomp, "binary",     vs)
  df_continuous = prepare_tmle_long_data(df_comparison, df_newcomp, "continuous", vs)
  
  cat("Binary observations:     ", nrow(df_binary), "\n")
  cat("Continuous observations: ", nrow(df_continuous), "\n\n")
  
  # Generate plots
  p_bin  = make_tmle_plot(df_binary,     "binary",     vs)
  p_cont = make_tmle_plot(df_continuous, "continuous", vs)
  
  ggsave(file.path(output_dir, paste0("tmle_comparison_binary_",     vs_tag, ".png")),
         p_bin,  width = pub_width, height = pub_height, dpi = pub_dpi, bg = "white")
  ggsave(file.path(output_dir, paste0("tmle_comparison_continuous_", vs_tag, ".png")),
         p_cont, width = pub_width, height = pub_height, dpi = pub_dpi, bg = "white")
  
  cat("Saved: tmle_comparison_binary_",     vs_tag, ".png\n", sep = "")
  cat("Saved: tmle_comparison_continuous_", vs_tag, ".png\n", sep = "")
  
  # Summary stats
  calc_stats = function(data) {
    data %>%
      filter(!is.na(pvr)) %>%
      group_by(method) %>%
      summarise(
        n          = n(),
        mean_pvr   = round(mean(pvr,               na.rm = TRUE), 4),
        median_pvr = round(median(pvr,             na.rm = TRUE), 4),
        sd_pvr     = round(sd(pvr,                 na.rm = TRUE), 4),
        q25        = round(quantile(pvr, 0.25,     na.rm = TRUE), 4),
        q75        = round(quantile(pvr, 0.75,     na.rm = TRUE), 4),
        .groups    = "drop"
      ) %>%
      mutate(vs_method = vs_labels[vs], outcome = NA)   # outcome filled below
  }
  
  bin_stats  = calc_stats(df_binary)     %>% mutate(outcome = "Binary")
  cont_stats = calc_stats(df_continuous) %>% mutate(outcome = "Continuous")
  
  all_stats[[vs]] = bind_rows(bin_stats, cont_stats)
  
  cat("\nBinary stats:\n");     print(bin_stats)
  cat("\nContinuous stats:\n"); print(cont_stats)
}

###############################################################################
##  Combined summary table ---------------------------------------------------
###############################################################################
cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("COMBINED SUMMARY TABLE\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")
combined_stats = bind_rows(all_stats) %>%
  select(vs_method, outcome, method, n, mean_pvr, median_pvr, sd_pvr, q25, q75)
print(combined_stats)

###############################################################################
##  File list ----------------------------------------------------------------
###############################################################################
cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("ALL PLOT FILES GENERATED (6 total):\n")
cat("========================================\n")
for (vs in vs_methods) {
  vs_tag = tolower(gsub("\\+", "plus", vs))
  cat("  tmle_comparison_binary_",     vs_tag, ".png\n", sep = "")
  cat("  tmle_comparison_continuous_", vs_tag, ".png\n", sep = "")
}
cat("========================================\n\n")

```

# N Hist
```{r}
# Create a data frame
df = data.frame(sample_size = df_meta$'Sample Size')

# Calculate summary statistics
mean_size = mean(df$sample_size)
median_size = median(df$sample_size)
n_studies = length(df$sample_size)

###############################################################################
## Plot 1: Original Linear Scale Histogram
###############################################################################

p = ggplot(df, aes(x = sample_size)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, 
                 fill = "#4CAF50", 
                 color = "white", 
                 alpha = 0.8,
                 boundary = 0) +
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept = mean_size), 
             color = "#FF5722", 
             linetype = "dashed", 
             linewidth = 1.2,
             alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept = median_size), 
             color = "#2196F3", 
             linetype = "dotted", 
             linewidth = 1.2,
             alpha = 0.8) +
  labs(x = "Sample Size",
       y = "Number of Studies") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "gray90", linewidth = 0.5),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA)
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::comma_format()) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0, 0.05, 0))

print(p)
# ggsave("cleaned_data/Plot/sample_size_linear.png", p, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 600, bg = "white")

###############################################################################
## Plot 2: Custom Bins (RECOMMENDED for skewed data)
###############################################################################

# Create custom bins with wider intervals for larger values
df_binned = df %>%
  mutate(
    size_category = cut(sample_size, 
                       breaks = c(0, 50, 100, 200, 500, 1000, Inf),
                       labels = c("0-50", "51-100", "101-200", 
                                 "201-500", "501-1000", "1000+"),
                       include.lowest = TRUE)
  )

# Count by category
bin_summary = df_binned %>%
  count(size_category) %>%
  mutate(percentage = round(n/sum(n)*100, 1))

p_custom = ggplot(df_binned, aes(x = size_category)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "#5B9BD5", alpha = 0.85, color = "white", width = 0.65) +
  geom_text(stat = "count", aes(label = after_stat(count)), 
            vjust = -0.5, size = 5, fontface = "bold", color = "gray20") +
  labs(x = "Sample Size Range",
       y = "Number of Studies") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(size = 22, face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "gray90", linewidth = 0.5),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA)
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0, 0.1, 0))

print(p_custom)

# Save PNG
ggsave("cleaned_data/Plot/sample_size_custom_bins.png", p_custom, 
       width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 600, bg = "white")

# Save PDF
ggsave("cleaned_data/Plot/sample_size_custom_bins.pdf", p_custom, 
       width = 10, height = 6, device = "pdf", bg = "white")

cat("✓ Saved PNG: cleaned_data/Plot/sample_size_custom_bins.png\n")
cat("✓ Saved PDF: cleaned_data/Plot/sample_size_custom_bins.pdf\n\n")

###############################################################################
## Plot 3: Square Root Transformation
###############################################################################

p_sqrt = ggplot(df, aes(x = sqrt(sample_size))) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 20, 
                 fill = "#FF9800", 
                 color = "white", 
                 alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = sqrt(c(0, 100, 500, 1000, 2000, 4000, 6000)),
    labels = scales::comma_format()(c(0, 100, 500, 1000, 2000, 4000, 6000))
  ) +
  labs(x = "Sample Size (√ scale)",
       y = "Number of Studies") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(size = 22, face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "gray90", linewidth = 0.5),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA)
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0, 0.05, 0))

print(p_sqrt)
# ggsave("cleaned_data/Plot/sample_size_sqrt.png", p_sqrt, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 600, bg = "white")

###############################################################################
## Plot 4: Log-Transformed Distribution
###############################################################################

p_log = ggplot(df, aes(x = log10(sample_size))) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 25, 
                 fill = "#9C27B0", 
                 color = "white", 
                 alpha = 0.8) +
  labs(x = "Log10(Sample Size)",
       y = "Number of Studies") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(size = 22, face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "gray90", linewidth = 0.5),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA)
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0, 0.05, 0))

print(p_log)
# ggsave("cleaned_data/Plot/sample_size_log.png", p_log, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 600, bg = "white")

###############################################################################
## Additional Analysis: Categorize Studies by Size
###############################################################################

df = df %>%
  mutate(size_category_detailed = case_when(
    sample_size < 100 ~ "Small (< 100)",
    sample_size >= 100 & sample_size < 500 ~ "Medium (100-499)",
    sample_size >= 500 & sample_size < 1000 ~ "Large (500-999)",
    sample_size >= 1000 ~ "Very Large (≥ 1000)"
  ))

# Print summary
cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("Sample Size Summary Statistics\n")
cat("========================================\n")
print(summary(df$sample_size))

cat("\n\nDistribution by Size Category:\n")
category_summary = df %>%
  count(size_category_detailed) %>%
  mutate(percentage = round(n/sum(n)*100, 1))
print(category_summary)

cat("\n\nDistribution by Custom Bins:\n")
print(bin_summary)

cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("Plot files saved to cleaned_data/Plot/\n")
cat("========================================\n")
```

```{r}
###############################################################################

# Prepare data
df_citation = data.frame(
  citation = df_meta$Citation
) %>%
  filter(!is.na(citation))

# Calculate summary statistics
mean_citation = mean(df_citation$citation)
median_citation = median(df_citation$citation)
n_studies = nrow(df_citation)

cat("Max:", max(df_citation$citation), "\n")

# Create custom bins balanced for citation distribution

df_citation_binned = df_citation %>%
  mutate(
    citation_category = cut(citation, 
                       breaks = c(0, 25, 50, 100, 200, 400, Inf),
                       labels = c("0-25", "26-50", "51-100", 
                                 "101-200", "201-400", "400+"),
                       include.lowest = TRUE)

  count(citation_category) %>%
  mutate(percentage = round(n/sum(n)*100, 1))

  geom_bar(fill = "#FFC000", alpha = 0.85, color = "white", width = 0.65) +

    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "gray90", linewidth = 0.5),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA)
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0, 0.1, 0))

# Display plot

# Save PNG

       width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 600, bg = "white")
```

# N. Cova vs N Plot
```{r}
###############################################################################
##  Covariates vs Sample Size - Scatter Plots Only
###############################################################################
# Prepare data: get mean covariates per trial and merge with sample size
df_cov_summary = df_comparison %>% 
  group_by(Trial_No) %>% 
  summarise(mean_covariates = round(mean(N_Covariates, na.rm = TRUE)), 
            .groups = 'drop')

# Merge with sample size data from df_meta (using Trial_ID)
df_cov_size = df_meta %>%
  select(Trial_ID, Sample_Size = `Sample Size`) %>%
  inner_join(df_cov_summary, by = c("Trial_ID" = "Trial_No")) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Sample_Size), !is.na(mean_covariates))

# Calculate correlation
correlation = cor(df_cov_size$Sample_Size, df_cov_size$mean_covariates, 
                  use = "complete.obs")

###############################################################################
## Original Scale Version (Scatter Only)
###############################################################################
p_cov_size = ggplot(df_cov_size, aes(x = Sample_Size, y = mean_covariates)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 3, color = "#2196F3") +
  labs(x = "Sample Size",
       y = "Average Number of Covariates") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 18) +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA)
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::comma_format())

# Display and save
print(p_cov_size)
ggsave("cleaned_data/Plot/covariates_vs_sample_size.png", p_cov_size, 
       width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 600, bg = "white")

###############################################################################
## Log10 Version (Scatter with reference curves)
###############################################################################
# Get the y-axis range from the original plot
y_range = range(df_cov_size$mean_covariates, na.rm = TRUE)

# Get the range for reference curves
log_sample_size_range = range(log10(df_cov_size$Sample_Size), na.rm = TRUE)
log_x_seq = seq(log_sample_size_range[1], log_sample_size_range[2], length.out = 200)

# Create reference curve data
ref_curve1 = data.frame(log_x = log_x_seq, y = 10^log_x_seq)
ref_curve2 = data.frame(log_x = log_x_seq, y = 10^log_x_seq / 5)

# Filter curves to stay within y-axis range
ref_curve1 = ref_curve1 %>% filter(y >= y_range[1], y <= y_range[2])
ref_curve2 = ref_curve2 %>% filter(y >= y_range[1], y <= y_range[2])

# Find good positions for labels (around 75% along each curve)
label_pos1 = ref_curve1[round(nrow(ref_curve1) * 0.75), ]
label_pos2 = ref_curve2[round(nrow(ref_curve2) * 0.75), ]

p_cov_size_log = ggplot(df_cov_size, aes(x = log10(Sample_Size), y = mean_covariates)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 3, color = "#2196F3") +
  geom_line(data = ref_curve1, 
            aes(x = log_x, y = y),
            color = "#E91E63", 
            linewidth = 1,
            inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  geom_line(data = ref_curve2, 
            aes(x = log_x, y = y),
            color = "#FF9800", 
            linewidth = 1,
            inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  annotate("text", x = label_pos1$log_x, y = label_pos1$y, 
           label = "n = p", color = "#E91E63", size = 5, fontface = "bold",
           hjust = -0.2, vjust = -0.5) +
  annotate("text", x = label_pos2$log_x, y = label_pos2$y, 
           label = "n = 5p", color = "#FF9800", size = 5, fontface = "bold",
           hjust = -0.2, vjust = 1.5) +
  labs(x = "Log10(Sample Size)",
       y = "Average Number of Covariates") +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = y_range) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 18) +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 16),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA)
  )

# Display and save log version
print(p_cov_size_log)
ggsave("cleaned_data/Plot/covariates_vs_sample_size_log.png", p_cov_size_log, 
       width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 600, bg = "white")

###############################################################################
## Summary Statistics
###############################################################################
cat("\n=== Covariates vs Sample Size Summary ===\n")
cat("Number of trials:", nrow(df_cov_size), "\n")
cat("Correlation coefficient:", round(correlation, 3), "\n")
cat("\nSample Size Summary:\n")
print(summary(df_cov_size$Sample_Size))
cat("\nNumber of Covariates Summary:\n")
print(summary(df_cov_size$mean_covariates))
cat("\nBoth plots saved to cleaned_data/Plot/\n")
cat("  - covariates_vs_sample_size.png (original scale)\n")
cat("  - covariates_vs_sample_size_log.png (log10 scale with reference curves)\n")
cat("\nReference curves on log plot:\n")
cat("  - n = p (pink): Sample size equals number of covariates\n")
cat("  - n = 5p (orange): Sample size equals 5 times number of covariates\n")
```

```{r}
# Consolidate similar research areas
df_meta_consolidated <- df_meta %>%
  mutate(Research_Area_Consolidated = case_when(
    `Research Area` %in% c("Anesthesiology") ~ "Anesthesiology",
    `Research Area` %in% c("HIV") ~ "HIV/AIDS",
    `Research Area` %in% c("Maternal and Child Health", "Obstetrics", "Neonatology") ~ "Maternal & Child Health",
    `Research Area` %in% c("Neurology", "Neuropsychology") ~ "Neurology",
    `Research Area` %in% c("COVID-19", "Tuberculosis", "Vaccinology") ~ "Infectious Disease",
    `Research Area` %in% c("Mental Health", "Addiction Medicine") ~ "Mental Health",
    `Research Area` %in% c("Cardiovascular Disease", "Hypertension") ~ "Cardiovascular",
    TRUE ~ `Research Area`
  ))

# Count and prepare data
area_counts <- df_meta_consolidated %>%
  count(Research_Area_Consolidated) %>%
  arrange(desc(n)) %>%
  mutate(
    percentage = n / sum(n) * 100,
    label = ifelse(percentage > 3, 
                   paste0(Research_Area_Consolidated, "\n(", n, ")"),
                   "")
  )

# Plot research area pie chart
p2 <- ggplot(area_counts, 
             aes(x = "", y = n, fill = Research_Area_Consolidated)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 1, color = "white", linewidth = 0.5) +
  coord_polar("y", start = 0) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "turbo") +

  theme(
    legend.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 18),
    legend.position = "right"
  ) +
  labs(fill = "Research Area") +
  geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(n >= 3, n, "")), 
            position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
            color = "white", size = 3.5, fontface = "bold")

# Display plot

cat("✓ Saved PDF: cleaned_data/Plot/research_area_pie.pdf\n\n")

# Print summary

cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("Plot files saved to cleaned_data/Plot/\n")
cat("Nature Communications quality: 600 dpi PNG + vector PDF\n")
cat("========================================\n")
```

# Processing Flowchart
```{r}
flowchart = grViz("
digraph data_curation {
  # ==== GLOBAL GRAPH SETTINGS ====
  graph [layout = dot, rankdir = TB, fontname = Helvetica, ranksep = 1.2, nodesep = 0.5]
  node  [shape = rectangle, style = filled, fontname = Helvetica, fontsize = 12, height = 1.2, width = 2.5]
  edge  [penwidth = 1.5]
  
  # ================== ROW 1: Left → Right (LIGHTBLUE) ==================
  {
    rank = same;
    
    search [label = 'Search Public Databases\\nKeywords: randomized controlled trial,\\nrandomized trial,\\nrandomized clinical trial',
            fillcolor = lightblue]
    
    databases [label = 'Databases:\\n• Dryad\\n• Harvard Dataverse\\n• Zenodo\\n\\nR Packages (CRAN)', 
               fillcolor = lightblue]
    
    criteria [label = 'Selection Criteria:\\n• Clear data dictionary\\n• Key variables present\\n• Published paper available\\n• Confirmed RCT design', 
              fillcolor = lightblue]
    
    datasets [label = '50 RCT Datasets:\\n• 38 from Dryad\\n• 2 from Harvard Dataverse\\n• 2 from Zenodo \\n• 8 from R packages', 
              fillcolor = lightblue]
  }
  
  # Row 1 connections (left to right)
  search -> databases -> criteria -> datasets
  
  # ================== ROW 2: Right → Left (LIGHTCORAL) ==================
  {
    rank = same;
    
    cleaning_start [label = 'Data Cleaning Process', 
                    fillcolor = lightcoral]
    
    extract [label = 'Extract Variables:\\n• Baseline covariates\\n• Treatment assignment\\n• Primary outcomes\\n• Secondary outcomes', 
             fillcolor = lightcoral]
    
    missing_std [label = 'Standardize Missing Values:\\nRename all to NA', 
                 fillcolor = lightcoral]
    
    reshape [label = 'Reshape Data:\\nConvert to wide format', 
             fillcolor = lightcoral]
  }
  
  # Row 2 connections (right to left)
  reshape -> missing_std -> extract -> cleaning_start [dir = back]
  
  # ================== ROW 3: Left → Right (PEACH) ==================
  {
    rank = same;
    
    missing_start [label = 'Missing Data Handling', 
                   fillcolor = '#FFE4B5']
    
    outcome_filter [label = 'Filter Outcomes:\\nRemove outcomes with\\nmissingness > 40%', 
                    fillcolor = '#FFE4B5']
    
    patient_filter [label = 'Remove Patients:\\nExclude patients with\\nmissing outcome values', 
                    fillcolor = '#FFE4B5']
    
    covariate_impute [label = 'Impute Covariates:\\n• Continuous → Mean imputation\\n• Categorical → Mode imputation', 
                      fillcolor = '#FFE4B5']
  }
  
  # Row 3 connections (left to right)
  missing_start -> outcome_filter -> patient_filter -> covariate_impute
  
  # ================== VERTICAL CONNECTIONS ==================
  # Row 1 to Row 2 (datasets → cleaning_start with arrow)
  datasets -> cleaning_start [weight = 0]
  
  # Row 2 to Row 3 (reshape → missing_start with arrow)
  reshape -> missing_start [weight = 0]
}
")
```

# Error Rate
## TMLE
### Summary Statistics
```{r}
###############################################################################
##  ERROR RATE AND MEAN/MEDIAN VARIANCE REDUCTION FOR INDIVIDUAL SL METHODS --
###############################################################################
# Individual SL methods (All covariates - base versions only, no VS1 or VS2)
individual_sl_methods = c("SL_glm", "SL_glmnet", "SL_rpart", "SL_gam", "SL_randomForest", "SL_nnet", 
                          "SL_svm", "SL_xgboost","SL_bartMachine")

###############################################################################
##  Function to calculate error rate and mean/median variance reduction -------
###############################################################################
calculate_sl_stats = function(data, outcome_type) {
  
  # Filter by outcome type
  if (outcome_type == "continuous") {
    data_filtered = data %>%
      filter(`Outcome Type` %in% c("continuous", "continuous proportion"))
  } else if (outcome_type == "binary") {
    data_filtered = data %>%
      filter(`Outcome Type` %in% c("binary", "composite binary"))
  }
  
  # Initialize results dataframe
  results = data.frame(
    method = character(),
    error_rate = numeric(),
    mean = numeric(),
    median = numeric(),
    sd = numeric(),
    n_total = integer(),
    n_valid = integer(),
    n_na = integer(),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  # Loop through each individual SL method
  for (method in individual_sl_methods) {
    # Get the precision gain column name for this method (base version only)
    pg_col = paste0("How much precision gain can ", method, "_TMLE provide?")
    
    # Check if column exists
    if (pg_col %in% colnames(data_filtered)) {
      
      # Extract precision gain values
      pg_values = data_filtered[[pg_col]]
      
      # Calculate statistics
      n_total = length(pg_values)
      n_na = sum(is.na(pg_values))
      error_rate = n_na / n_total
      n_valid = n_total - n_na
      
      # Calculate mean, median, and SD of variance reduction (1 - precision_gain), excluding NA
      if (n_valid > 0) {
        var_reduction = 1 - pg_values
        mean_var_reduction = mean(var_reduction, na.rm = TRUE)
        median_var_reduction = median(var_reduction, na.rm = TRUE)
        sd_var_reduction = sd(var_reduction, na.rm = TRUE)
      } else {
        mean_var_reduction = NA
        median_var_reduction = NA
        sd_var_reduction = NA
      }
      
      # Add to results
      results = rbind(results, data.frame(
        method = method,
        error_rate = error_rate,
        mean = mean_var_reduction,
        median = median_var_reduction,
        sd = sd_var_reduction,
        n_total = n_total,
        n_valid = n_valid,
        n_na = n_na,
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      ))
    }
  }
  
  # Round numeric columns for better display
  results = results %>%
    mutate(
      error_rate = round(error_rate, 2),
      mean = round(mean, 4),
      median = round(median, 4),
      sd = round(sd, 4)
    )
  
  return(results)
}


###############################################################################
##  Calculate and display results with enhanced formatting -------------------
###############################################################################
# Binary outcomes - All outcomes
cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("BINARY OUTCOMES - ALL OUTCOMES\n")
cat("Individual SL Methods (All Covariates)\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

binary_all_sl_stats = calculate_sl_stats(df_comparison, "binary")

# Table 1: Error Rate Statistics
cat("TABLE 1: ERROR RATE STATISTICS\n")
cat("----------------------------------------\n\n")
print(binary_all_sl_stats[, c("method", "error_rate", "n_total", "n_valid", "n_na")])

# Table 2: Variance Reduction Statistics
cat("\n\nTABLE 2: VARIANCE REDUCTION STATISTICS\n")
cat("----------------------------------------\n\n")
print(binary_all_sl_stats[, c("method", "mean", "sd", "median")])

# Continuous outcomes - All outcomes
cat("\n\n========================================\n")
cat("CONTINUOUS OUTCOMES - ALL OUTCOMES\n")
cat("Individual SL Methods (All Covariates)\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

continuous_all_sl_stats = calculate_sl_stats(df_comparison, "continuous")

# Table 1: Error Rate Statistics
cat("TABLE 1: ERROR RATE STATISTICS\n")
cat("----------------------------------------\n\n")
print(continuous_all_sl_stats[, c("method", "error_rate", "n_total", "n_valid", "n_na")])

# Table 2: Variance Reduction Statistics
cat("\n\nTABLE 2: VARIANCE REDUCTION STATISTICS\n")
cat("----------------------------------------\n\n")
print(continuous_all_sl_stats[, c("method", "mean", "sd", "median")])
```
### Boxplot
```{r}
###############################################################################
##  BOXPLOTS FOR INDIVIDUAL ML METHODS - PVR ANALYSIS ------------------------
##  Mimics main analysis structure with All Outcomes and Primary Outcomes
##  SELECTIVE outlier removal for continuous outcomes only:
##    - SL_glm, SL_gam, SL_xgboost: pg >= 5 removed
##  Individual SL methods only use "All covariates" (no Top-3 or Baseline+)
###############################################################################

# Individual SL methods (All covariates - base versions only, no VS1 or VS2)
individual_sl_methods = c("SL_glm", "SL_glmnet", "SL_rpart", "SL_gam", "SL_randomForest", 
                          "SL_nnet", "SL_svm", "SL_xgboost", "SL_bartMachine")

# Manual outlier threshold
pg_manual_threshold = 2

# Methods that get outlier removal for continuous outcomes
continuous_outlier_methods = c("SL_glm", "SL_gam", "SL_xgboost")

###############################################################################
##  Function to prepare long data for individual ML methods ------------------
###############################################################################
prepare_ml_long_data = function(data, outcome_type) {
  
  # Filter by outcome type
  if (outcome_type == "continuous") {
    data_filtered = data %>%
      filter(`Outcome Type` %in% c("continuous", "continuous proportion"))
  } else if (outcome_type == "binary") {
    data_filtered = data %>%
      filter(`Outcome Type` %in% c("binary", "composite binary"))
  }
  
  # Initialize list to store data for each method
  all_method_data = list()
  
  # Loop through each individual SL method
  for (method in individual_sl_methods) {
    # Get the precision gain column name for this method (base version only)
    pg_col = paste0("How much precision gain can ", method, "_TMLE provide?")
    
    # Check if column exists
    if (pg_col %in% colnames(data_filtered)) {
      # Extract precision gain values and create dataframe
      method_data = data.frame(
        method = method,
        pg = data_filtered[[pg_col]],
        pvr = 1 - data_filtered[[pg_col]],  # Proportional Variance Reduction
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
      
      all_method_data[[length(all_method_data) + 1]] = method_data
    }
  }
  
  # Combine all methods into one dataframe
  if (length(all_method_data) > 0) {
    result = bind_rows(all_method_data)
    return(result)
  } else {
    return(data.frame())
  }
}

###############################################################################
##  SELECTIVE OUTLIER REMOVAL FUNCTION ---------------------------------------
##  Removes outliers for SL_glm, SL_gam, SL_xgboost in continuous outcomes
###############################################################################
apply_selective_outlier_removal_ml = function(data, outcome_type) {
  if (outcome_type == "continuous") {
    # For continuous outcomes: remove outliers for specific methods
    data %>%
      mutate(pg = case_when(
        method %in% continuous_outlier_methods & pg >= pg_manual_threshold ~ NA_real_,
        TRUE ~ pg
      )) %>%
      mutate(pvr = 1 - pg)  # Recalculate PVR after outlier removal
  } else {
    # For binary outcomes: no outlier removal
    data
  }
}

###############################################################################
##  Color palette for individual ML methods ----------------------------------
###############################################################################
ml_method_colors = c(
  "SL_glm"           = "#F8766D",    # Red
  "SL_glmnet"        = "#B79F00",    # Gold
  "SL_rpart"         = "#00BA38",    # Green
  "SL_gam"           = "#00BFC4",    # Cyan
  "SL_randomForest"  = "#619CFF",    # Blue
  "SL_nnet"          = "#C77CFF",    # Purple
  "SL_svm"           = "#F564E3",    # Magenta
  "SL_xgboost"       = "#00C19F",    # Teal
  "SL_bartMachine"   = "#FF6E42"     # Orange-red
)

###############################################################################
##  Plot function for individual ML methods ----------------------------------
###############################################################################
make_ml_plot = function(data, outcome_type) {
  
  # Filter out NA values
  data = data %>% filter(!is.na(pvr))
  
  # Set factor levels to match the order in individual_sl_methods
  data = data %>%
    mutate(method = factor(method, levels = individual_sl_methods))
  
  # Counts for labels
  method_counts = data %>%
    group_by(method) %>%
    summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop")
  
  # x-axis label positions
  available_methods = individual_sl_methods[individual_sl_methods %in% unique(data$method)]
  x_labels_df = data.frame(
    method = available_methods,
    x_pos  = 1:length(available_methods)
  )
  
  x_labels_counts = x_labels_df %>%
    left_join(method_counts, by = "method")
  
  # Clean method names for display (remove "SL_" prefix)
  x_labels_counts = x_labels_counts %>%
    mutate(display_name = gsub("^SL_", "", method))
  
  # Create plot
  p = ggplot(data, aes(method, pvr, fill = method)) +
    geom_boxplot(colour = "grey25", alpha = .50, width = 0.5, outlier.shape = NA) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = ml_method_colors) +
    labs(x = NULL, y = "Proportional Variance Reduction") +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-1, 1), clip = "off") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 18) +
    theme(
      axis.text.x   = element_blank(),
      axis.ticks.x  = element_blank(),
      axis.text.y   = element_text(size = 16),
      axis.title.y  = element_text(size = 18),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
      plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
      legend.position  = "none",
      plot.margin      = margin(t = 5, r = 10, b = 60, l = 10)
    ) +
    geom_text(data = x_labels_counts, 
              aes(x = x_pos, y = -1.15, label = display_name),
              inherit.aes = FALSE, size = 5.5, lineheight = 0.85, hjust = 0.5) +
    geom_text(data = x_labels_counts, 
              aes(x = x_pos, y = -1.35, label = paste0("(", n, ")")),
              inherit.aes = FALSE, size = 4.5, hjust = 0.5)
  
  return(p)
}

###############################################################################
##  Publication quality settings ---------------------------------------------
###############################################################################
pub_width  = 12   # Slightly wider to accommodate 9 methods
pub_height = 6.5
pub_dpi    = 600

###############################################################################
##  Create output directory --------------------------------------------------
###############################################################################
output_dir = "cleaned_data/plot/exam plot"
dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

###############################################################################
##  Prepare data for ALL OUTCOMES --------------------------------------------
###############################################################################

df_ml_continuous_all_full = prepare_ml_long_data(df_comparison, "continuous")

df_ml_continuous_all = apply_selective_outlier_removal_ml(df_ml_continuous_all_full, "continuous")

  filter(str_detect(Outcome, "^YP_"))

df_ml_continuous_primary_full = prepare_ml_long_data(df_comparison_primary, "continuous")

df_ml_continuous_primary = apply_selective_outlier_removal_ml(df_ml_continuous_primary_full, "continuous")

       p_binary_all, width = pub_width, height = pub_height, dpi = pub_dpi, bg = "white")

###############################################################################
##  Summary Statistics -------------------------------------------------------
###############################################################################
cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("SUMMARY STATISTICS\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

# Function to calculate summary stats
calculate_summary_stats = function(data, data_label) {
  
  # Filter out NA values
  data_clean = data %>% filter(!is.na(pvr))
  
  stats = data_clean %>%
    group_by(method) %>%
    summarise(
      n = n(),
      mean_pvr = mean(pvr, na.rm = TRUE),
      median_pvr = median(pvr, na.rm = TRUE),
      sd_pvr = sd(pvr, na.rm = TRUE),
      q25 = quantile(pvr, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
      q75 = quantile(pvr, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(across(c(mean_pvr, median_pvr, sd_pvr, q25, q75), ~ round(., 4)))
  
  return(stats)
}

# Binary outcomes - All
cat("BINARY OUTCOMES - ALL OUTCOMES\n")
cat("----------------------------------------\n\n")
binary_stats_all = calculate_summary_stats(df_ml_binary_all, "all")
print(binary_stats_all)

# Binary outcomes - Primary

cat("----------------------------------------\n\n")

print(binary_stats_primary)

# Continuous outcomes - All

    outliers = sum(pg >= pg_manual_threshold),

  summarise(

    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(outliers))
```

## AIPW
```{r}
###############################################################################
##  ERROR RATE AND MEAN/MEDIAN VARIANCE REDUCTION FOR INDIVIDUAL SL METHODS --
###############################################################################
# Individual SL methods (All covariates - base versions only, no VS1 or VS2)
individual_sl_methods = c("SL_glm", "SL_glmnet", "SL_rpart", "SL_gam", "SL_nnet", 
                          "SL_svm", "SL_xgboost","SL_bartMachine", "SL_randomForest")

###############################################################################
##  Function to calculate error rate and mean/median variance reduction -------
###############################################################################
calculate_sl_stats = function(data, outcome_type) {
  
  # Filter by outcome type
  if (outcome_type == "continuous") {
    data_filtered = data %>%
      filter(`Outcome Type` %in% c("continuous", "continuous proportion"))
  } else if (outcome_type == "binary") {
    data_filtered = data %>%
      filter(`Outcome Type` %in% c("binary", "composite binary"))
  }
  
  # Initialize results dataframe
  results = data.frame(
    method = character(),
    error_rate = numeric(),
    mean_variance_reduction = numeric(),
    median_variance_reduction = numeric(),  # Added median column
    n_total = integer(),
    n_valid = integer(),
    n_na = integer(),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  # Loop through each individual SL method
  for (method in individual_sl_methods) {
    # Get the precision gain column name for this method (base version only)
    pg_col = paste0("How much precision gain can ", method, "_AIPW provide?")
    
    # Check if column exists
    if (pg_col %in% colnames(data_filtered)) {
      
      # Extract precision gain values
      pg_values = data_filtered[[pg_col]]
      
      # Calculate statistics
      n_total = length(pg_values)
      n_na = sum(is.na(pg_values))
      error_rate = n_na / n_total
      n_valid = n_total - n_na
      
      # Calculate mean and median variance reduction (1 - precision_gain), excluding NA
      if (n_valid > 0) {
        var_reduction = 1 - pg_values
        mean_var_reduction = mean(var_reduction, na.rm = TRUE)
        median_var_reduction = median(var_reduction, na.rm = TRUE)  # Calculate median
      } else {
        mean_var_reduction = NA
        median_var_reduction = NA
      }
      
      # Add to results
      results = rbind(results, data.frame(
        method = method,
        error_rate = error_rate,
        mean_variance_reduction = mean_var_reduction,
        median_variance_reduction = median_var_reduction,  # Add median to results
        n_total = n_total,
        n_valid = n_valid,
        n_na = n_na,
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      ))
    }
  }
  
  # Round numeric columns for better display
  results = results %>%
    mutate(
      error_rate = round(error_rate, 2),
      mean_variance_reduction = round(mean_variance_reduction, 4),
      median_variance_reduction = round(median_variance_reduction, 4)  # Round median
    )
  
  return(results)
}


###############################################################################
##  Calculate and display results with enhanced formatting -------------------
###############################################################################
# Binary outcomes - All outcomes
cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("BINARY OUTCOMES - ALL OUTCOMES\n")
cat("Individual SL Methods (All Covariates)\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

binary_all_sl_stats = calculate_sl_stats(df_comparison, "binary")
# Display with desired column order
print(binary_all_sl_stats[, c("method", "error_rate", "mean_variance_reduction", 
                                "median_variance_reduction", "n_total", "n_valid", "n_na")])

# Continuous outcomes - All outcomes
cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("CONTINUOUS OUTCOMES - ALL OUTCOMES\n")
cat("Individual SL Methods (All Covariates)\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

continuous_all_sl_stats = calculate_sl_stats(df_comparison, "continuous")
# Display with desired column order
print(continuous_all_sl_stats[, c("method", "error_rate", "mean_variance_reduction", 
                                   "median_variance_reduction", "n_total", "n_valid", "n_na")])
```


# CAG & CAL
```{r}
###############################################################################
##  CAG AND CAL CALCULATION - PRIMARY OUTCOMES -------------------------------
###############################################################################

# Function to calculate CAG and CAL for a given method and outcome type
calculate_cag_cal = function(data, outcome_type, method_name, adjustment_type = "All") {
  
  # Filter by outcome type
  if (outcome_type == "continuous") {
    data_filtered = data %>%
      filter(`Outcome Type` %in% c("continuous", "continuous proportion"))
  } else if (outcome_type == "binary") {
    data_filtered = data %>%
      filter(`Outcome Type` %in% c("binary", "composite binary"))
  } else if (outcome_type == "overall") {
    # Include all outcome types for overall calculation
    data_filtered = data
  }
  
  # Construct column names based on adjustment type
  if (adjustment_type == "All") {
    pval_col = paste0(method_name, "_pval")
  } else if (adjustment_type == "Top-3") {
    pval_col = paste0(method_name, "_VS1_pval")
  } else if (adjustment_type == "Baseline+") {
    pval_col = paste0(method_name, "_VS2_pval")
  }
  
  # Check if columns exist
  if (!("Unadjust_pval" %in% colnames(data_filtered)) || 
      !(pval_col %in% colnames(data_filtered))) {
    return(list(CAG = NA, CAL = NA, n = 0))
  }
  
  # Get p-values
  unadjust_pval = data_filtered$Unadjust_pval
  adjusted_pval = data_filtered[[pval_col]]
  
  # Filter to cases where both p-values are non-NA
  valid_cases = !is.na(unadjust_pval) & !is.na(adjusted_pval)
  unadjust_pval = unadjust_pval[valid_cases]
  adjusted_pval = adjusted_pval[valid_cases]
  
  n_total = length(unadjust_pval)
  
  # Calculate CAG: unadjusted is NOT significant (>= 0.05) AND adjusted IS significant (< 0.05)
  cag_cases = (unadjust_pval >= 0.05) & (adjusted_pval < 0.05)
  cag_count = sum(cag_cases)
  cag_probability = cag_count / n_total  # as proportion
  
  # Calculate CAL: unadjusted IS significant (< 0.05) AND adjusted is NOT significant (>= 0.05)
  cal_cases = (unadjust_pval < 0.05) & (adjusted_pval >= 0.05)
  cal_count = sum(cal_cases)
  cal_probability = cal_count / n_total  # as proportion
  
  return(list(CAG = cag_probability, CAL = cal_probability, n = n_total))
}

###############################################################################
##  ALL OUTCOMES - Build the complete table ---------------------------------
###############################################################################

# Define methods and their names in the data - REORDERED TO MATCH PLOT
methods = data.frame(
  display_name = c("ANCOVA", "ANHECOVA", "DML", "TMLE", "IPW", "G-Logistic"),
  data_name = c("ANCOVA", "ANHECOVA", "DML", "TMLE", "IPW", "Logistic G-Computation"),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Define adjustment types - REORDERED TO MATCH PLOT
adjustment_types = c("All", "Top-3", "Baseline+")

# Initialize results dataframe
results_all = data.frame(
  Method = character(),
  `Covariate selection` = character(),
  Continuous_CAG = numeric(),
  Continuous_CAL = numeric(),
  Binary_CAG = numeric(),
  Binary_CAL = numeric(),
  Overall_CAG = numeric(),
  Overall_CAL = numeric(),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
  check.names = FALSE
)

# Loop through methods and adjustment types
for (i in 1:nrow(methods)) {
  method_display = methods$display_name[i]
  method_data = methods$data_name[i]
  
  for (adj_type in adjustment_types) {
    # Calculate for continuous outcomes
    cont_results = calculate_cag_cal(df_comparison, "continuous", method_data, adj_type)
    
    # Calculate for binary outcomes
    binary_results = calculate_cag_cal(df_comparison, "binary", method_data, adj_type)
    
    # Calculate for overall (all outcomes combined)
    overall_results = calculate_cag_cal(df_comparison, "overall", method_data, adj_type)
    
    # Add to results
    results_all = rbind(results_all, data.frame(
      Method = method_display,
      `Covariate selection` = adj_type,
      Continuous_CAG = cont_results$CAG,
      Continuous_CAL = cont_results$CAL,
      Binary_CAG = binary_results$CAG,
      Binary_CAL = binary_results$CAL,
      Overall_CAG = overall_results$CAG,
      Overall_CAL = overall_results$CAL,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
      check.names = FALSE
    ))
  }
}

# Round to 2 decimal places
results_all = results_all %>%
  mutate(
    Continuous_CAG = round(Continuous_CAG, 2),
    Continuous_CAL = round(Continuous_CAL, 2),
    Binary_CAG = round(Binary_CAG, 2),
    Binary_CAL = round(Binary_CAL, 2),
    Overall_CAG = round(Overall_CAG, 2),
    Overall_CAL = round(Overall_CAL, 2)
  )

# Print the table
cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("CAG AND CAL TABLE - ALL OUTCOMES\n")
cat("Values are proportions (not percentages)\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")
print(results_all)

# Optional: Create a more formatted version for display
results_all_formatted = results_all %>%
  mutate(
    `Continuous outcome` = paste0(sprintf("%.2f", Continuous_CAG), " / ", 
                                   sprintf("%.2f", Continuous_CAL)),
    `Binary outcome` = paste0(sprintf("%.2f", Binary_CAG), " / ", 
                              sprintf("%.2f", Binary_CAL)),
    `Overall` = paste0(sprintf("%.2f", Overall_CAG), " / ", 
                       sprintf("%.2f", Overall_CAL))
  ) %>%
  select(Method, `Covariate selection`, `Continuous outcome`, `Binary outcome`, `Overall`)

cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("CAG AND CAL TABLE - ALL OUTCOMES (FORMATTED)\n")
cat("CAG / CAL format (proportions)\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")
print(results_all_formatted)

###############################################################################
##  ALL OUTCOMES - GRAND OVERALL CAG/CAL -------------------------------------
###############################################################################

cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("GRAND OVERALL CAG AND CAL - ALL OUTCOMES\n")
cat("(Across ALL methods, ALL covariate selections, ALL outcomes)\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

# Calculate mean CAG and CAL across all rows for each outcome type
grand_overall_all = results_all %>%
  summarise(
    Continuous_CAG_mean = mean(Continuous_CAG, na.rm = TRUE),
    Continuous_CAL_mean = mean(Continuous_CAL, na.rm = TRUE),
    Binary_CAG_mean = mean(Binary_CAG, na.rm = TRUE),
    Binary_CAL_mean = mean(Binary_CAL, na.rm = TRUE),
    Overall_CAG_mean = mean(Overall_CAG, na.rm = TRUE),
    Overall_CAL_mean = mean(Overall_CAL, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), ~round(., 3)))

cat("Mean CAG/CAL across all methods and covariate selections:\n\n")
cat(sprintf("Continuous outcomes: CAG = %.3f, CAL = %.3f\n", 
            grand_overall_all$Continuous_CAG_mean, grand_overall_all$Continuous_CAL_mean))
cat(sprintf("Binary outcomes:     CAG = %.3f, CAL = %.3f\n", 
            grand_overall_all$Binary_CAG_mean, grand_overall_all$Binary_CAL_mean))
cat(sprintf("Overall (all):       CAG = %.3f, CAL = %.3f\n\n", 
            grand_overall_all$Overall_CAG_mean, grand_overall_all$Overall_CAL_mean))

# Also calculate by covariate selection method (aggregated across all methods and outcomes)
by_selection_all = results_all %>%
  group_by(`Covariate selection`) %>%
  summarise(
    CAG_mean = mean(Overall_CAG, na.rm = TRUE),
    CAL_mean = mean(Overall_CAL, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(across(c(CAG_mean, CAL_mean), ~round(., 3)))

cat("========================================\n")
cat("By Covariate Selection Method:\n")
cat("(Averaged across all methods and outcomes)\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")
print(by_selection_all)

# Calculate by method (aggregated across all covariate selections and outcomes)
# Order by the original method order
by_method_all = results_all %>%
  group_by(Method) %>%
  summarise(
    CAG_mean = mean(Overall_CAG, na.rm = TRUE),
    CAL_mean = mean(Overall_CAL, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Method = factor(Method, levels = c("ANCOVA", "ANHECOVA", "DML", "TMLE", "IPW", "G-Logistic"))
  ) %>%
  arrange(Method) %>%
  mutate(across(c(CAG_mean, CAL_mean), ~round(., 3)))

cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("By Method:\n")
cat("(Averaged across all covariate selections and outcomes)\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")
print(by_method_all)

cat("\n========================================\n\n")

###############################################################################
##  PRIMARY OUTCOMES - Build the complete table -----------------------------
###############################################################################

# Filter df_comparison to primary outcomes only
df_comparison_primary = df_comparison %>%
  filter(str_detect(Outcome, "^YP_"))

cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("PRIMARY OUTCOMES FILTERING\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")
cat(sprintf("Total outcomes in df_comparison: %d\n", nrow(df_comparison)))
cat(sprintf("Primary outcomes (YP_ prefix): %d\n\n", nrow(df_comparison_primary)))

  Method = character(),
  `Covariate selection` = character(),
  Continuous_CAG = numeric(),
  Continuous_CAL = numeric(),
  Binary_CAG = numeric(),
  Binary_CAL = numeric(),
  Overall_CAG = numeric(),
  Overall_CAL = numeric(),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
  check.names = FALSE
)

# Loop through methods and adjustment types
for (i in 1:nrow(methods)) {
  method_display = methods$display_name[i]
  method_data = methods$data_name[i]
  
  for (adj_type in adjustment_types) {
    # Calculate for continuous outcomes
    cont_results = calculate_cag_cal(df_comparison_primary, "continuous", method_data, adj_type)
    
    # Calculate for binary outcomes
    binary_results = calculate_cag_cal(df_comparison_primary, "binary", method_data, adj_type)
    
    # Calculate for overall (all outcomes combined)
    overall_results = calculate_cag_cal(df_comparison_primary, "overall", method_data, adj_type)
    
    # Add to results

      Method = method_display,
      `Covariate selection` = adj_type,
      Continuous_CAG = cont_results$CAG,
      Continuous_CAL = cont_results$CAL,
      Binary_CAG = binary_results$CAG,
      Binary_CAL = binary_results$CAL,
      Overall_CAG = overall_results$CAG,
      Overall_CAL = overall_results$CAL,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
      check.names = FALSE
    ))
  }
}

# Round to 2 decimal places
results_primary = results_primary %>%
  mutate(
    Continuous_CAG = round(Continuous_CAG, 2),
    Continuous_CAL = round(Continuous_CAL, 2),
    Binary_CAG = round(Binary_CAG, 2),

cat("Values are proportions (not percentages)\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")
print(results_primary)

# Optional: Create a more formatted version for display

  mutate(
    `Continuous outcome` = paste0(sprintf("%.2f", Continuous_CAG), " / ", 
                                   sprintf("%.2f", Continuous_CAL)),
    `Binary outcome` = paste0(sprintf("%.2f", Binary_CAG), " / ", 
                              sprintf("%.2f", Binary_CAL)),
    `Overall` = paste0(sprintf("%.2f", Overall_CAG), " / ", 
                       sprintf("%.2f", Overall_CAL))
  ) %>%
  select(Method, `Covariate selection`, `Continuous outcome`, `Binary outcome`, `Overall`)

cat("\n========================================\n")

###############################################################################
##  PRIMARY OUTCOMES - GRAND OVERALL CAG/CAL ---------------------------------
###############################################################################

cat("========================================\n")
cat("By Covariate Selection Method:\n")
cat("(Averaged across all methods and primary outcomes)\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")
print(by_selection_primary)

# Calculate by method (aggregated across all covariate selections and outcomes)
# Order by the original method order
by_method_primary = results_primary %>%
  group_by(Method) %>%
  summarise(
    CAG_mean = mean(Overall_CAG, na.rm = TRUE),
    CAL_mean = mean(Overall_CAL, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Method = factor(Method, levels = c("ANCOVA", "ANHECOVA", "DML", "TMLE", "IPW", "G-Logistic"))
  ) %>%
  arrange(Method) %>%
  mutate(across(c(CAG_mean, CAL_mean), ~round(., 3)))

cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("By Method:\n")
cat("(Averaged across all covariate selections and primary outcomes)\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")
print(by_method_primary)

cat("\n========================================\n\n")
```
